Mimir Compactor PVC Pressure

Forensic execution trace: Grafana alert to governed GitOps remediation

Author

Observability Platform Engineering

Published

July 12, 2026

This document traces one concrete event: Grafana detects pressure on the Mimir compactor PVC, Kestra orchestrates a bounded workflow, the planner gateway authorizes a single draft GitOps pull request, and Argo CD later observes the approved repository revision. It describes the actual interfaces and durable state, not a conceptual product diagram.

The complete path

NoteEvidence and redaction policy

The guided trace uses a stable repository fixture so every canonical preimage and identity hash can be recomputed exactly. A separate live-sandbox cross-check publishes its real Kestra execution id, GitHub pull request, database keys, outbox counters, and audit-chain hashes. Active webhook keys, gateway bearer tokens, provider credentials, and authorization headers remain redacted while the sandbox is running. kestra.example.com is a neutral hostname placeholder.

ImportantThe trigger is not the reconciliation sweep

The initial compactor event is delivered by Grafana to the Kestra webhook. reconciliation_sweep_aiops.yml runs later. Its job is to surface incomplete, failed, or ambiguous durable operations that need recovery. It does not inspect PVC pressure and does not select this catalog entry.

What runs where

Component Runtime location Reads Writes Authority
Grafana alerting Global observability cell Metrics and alert rules Webhook event Detect and notify only
Kestra Platform automation namespace Webhook body and gateway responses Workflow execution state Choose the next task; no production mutation authority
Planner gateway Kubernetes Deployment, normally two or more replicas Catalog, request components, Postgres, approved provider APIs Durable state and bounded provider calls Enforce contracts, identity, policy, approval, and side-effect rules
Claude External API reached by the gateway planner provider Sanitized evidence plus bounded catalog context Typed plan response Propose only; cannot authorize or execute
OPA Gateway policy boundary Evidence, plan, catalog, runtime posture Allow/deny decision Deny unsafe proposals; cannot perform a change
Postgres Managed database, separate gateway schema Workflow state and outbox rows Attempts, outbox, audit, compensation Source of truth for idempotency and recovery
GitHub Enterprise Git service Current target branch and file Draft branch, commit, pull request Records the proposed configuration change
Human reviewer Existing pull-request process Diff, evidence, policy, ownership Approval and merge decision Final change authorization
Argo CD Existing GitOps control plane Approved Git revision Kubernetes reconciliation Applies merged desired state; gateway access is read-only

The planner gateway is an ordinary HTTP service. Claude is not deployed as a second Kubernetes agent: the gateway makes an API call to the configured model provider and validates the response against the plan contract.

Credential boundaries

Credential Held by Accepted by Purpose
Kestra webhook key Grafana contact point Kestra webhook trigger Permit alert ingestion for one flow
Gateway caller token Kestra secret store Planner gateway Authenticate and scope internal workflow calls
Anthropic or Bedrock credential Planner gateway secret/provider identity Model provider Obtain a typed plan proposal
GitHub App/private-key material Planner gateway secret/provider identity GitHub Read authoritative base and create a draft PR
Argo/Kubernetes service account Planner gateway pod Kubernetes API Read selected Argo Application resources only
Postgres credential Kestra or gateway, using separate users/schemas Managed Postgres Persist runtime state within the caller’s schema

The Grafana webhook never carries model, GitHub, Kubernetes, or database credentials. Kestra does not receive the model-provider credential. Claude does not receive any provider credential.

1. Trigger configuration

Grafana routing

Grafana is configured to send this alert through a webhook contact point:

Caller Receiver Protocol and operation Data transferred Receiver behavior
Grafana alert evaluator Grafana notification policy Internal alert-state transition Firing alert instance and labels Selects receiver aiops-kestra-webhook
Grafana contact point Kestra webhook trigger HTTPS POST Alertmanager-compatible v4 JSON Authenticates the webhook key and creates one flow execution
Kestra webhook trigger Kestra execution engine Internal trigger dispatch Raw webhook body as trigger data Returns quickly because wait: false; workflow continues asynchronously

The contact-point URL has this shape:

https://kestra.example.com/api/v1/main/executions/webhook/aiops.kestra/catalog_remediation_aiops/<secret-webhook-key>

The webhook key is an ingress credential. It should be high entropy, stored in the Kestra secret backend, rotated, and excluded from logs and screenshots. It is not the gateway shared secret used on internal Kestra-to-gateway calls.

Kestra trigger

The matching trigger is at the bottom of flows/catalog_remediation_aiops.yml:

triggers:
  - id: alertmanager_webhook
    type: io.kestra.plugin.core.trigger.Webhook
    key: __KESTRA_WEBHOOK_KEY__
    wait: false
    returnOutputs: false

wait: false means Kestra accepts the webhook without waiting for the full remediation. Grafana is not coupled to planner, approval, GitHub, or Argo latency. If the workflow later fails, that failure belongs to Kestra and the gateway recovery path; it is not returned as a long-running webhook response.

The flow also limits active executions and queues excess work:

concurrency:
  limit: 8
  behavior: QUEUE

2. Exact incoming event

The repository fixture is fixtures/grafana-webhook-mimir-compactor.json. A representative delivery is:

curl -X POST \
  "https://kestra.example.com/api/v1/main/executions/webhook/aiops.kestra/catalog_remediation_aiops/${KESTRA_WEBHOOK_KEY}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  --data @examples/kestra-aiops/fixtures/grafana-webhook-mimir-compactor.json
{
  "version": "4",
  "receiver": "aiops-kestra-webhook",
  "status": "firing",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "alerts": [
    {
      "status": "firing",
      "fingerprint": "grafana-labels-fingerprint-9d8c7b6a",
      "startsAt": "2026-07-01T18:00:00Z",
      "labels": {
        "alertname": "MimirCompactorPVCPressure",
        "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
        "cell_id": "contextual-cell-a",
        "namespace": "mimir",
        "workload": "mimir-compactor",
        "persistentvolumeclaim": "mimir-compactor-data",
        "severity": "warning"
      },
      "annotations": {
        "summary": "Mimir compactor PVC pressure",
        "description": "Mimir compactor PVC has less than 15 percent free capacity."
      }
    }
  ]
}

Field ownership

Field Produced by Used for Does not mean
fingerprint Grafana Source alert identity input Permission to create a PR
catalogEntryId Alert rule/contact-point integration Select the bounded remediation contract The alert chose 300Gi
cluster Alert labels Cell-group/cluster scope and target path Gateway has cluster-admin access
cell_id Alert labels Cell topology and audit scope A separate in-cell agent or model process exists
namespace Alert labels Resource and GitOps path scope Direct namespace mutation is allowed
persistentvolumeclaim Alert labels Resource identity PVC resize is automatically safe
status: firing Grafana Episode state The proposed remediation is correct

The event identifies the problem and its topology. It does not carry authority, an approved target value, credentials, a shell command, or a Kubernetes patch.

One webhook delivery can contain one alert or a group

Grafana sends one notification group per webhook delivery. The alerts array is therefore allowed to contain one or many alert instances. Every instance can carry its own native Grafana fingerprint.

Incoming shape Gateway source material Public episode key
One alert Sorted JSON array containing one normalized fingerprint alert:v1:<first-128-bits-of-SHA-256>
Two or more alerts Sorted JSON array containing every normalized fingerprint group:v1:<first-128-bits-of-SHA-256>
No native fingerprint Synthetic fingerprint derived from that alert’s canonical labels alert:v1:... or group:v1:..., kind synthetic or mixed

For this single-alert fixture, the canonical JSON value, formatted for reading, is:

[
  "native:grafana-labels-fingerprint-9d8c7b6a"
]

For a hypothetical two-alert delivery, the gateway sorts first, independent of the array order received from Grafana:

[
  "native:grafana-labels-fingerprint-1a2b",
  "native:grafana-labels-fingerprint-9d8c"
]

If grouped alerts disagree on topology fields that the selected catalog uses for identity, the gateway marks topologyAmbiguous: true; policy should stop the automation rather than silently choose one target. A group key still identifies only the source episode. It is not reused as the PR, approval, or outbox key.

3. The catalog contract

The selected catalog file is planner-gateway/catalog/remediations/mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure.yaml. The important fields are:

id: mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure
component: mimir-compactor
statefulness: stateful
riskRating: medium

requiredTopology:
  - cellGroup
  - cluster
  - namespace
  - resource

mutationBoundary: gitops_pull_request
allowedPrActions:
  - update_helm_value
  - increase_pvc_size
  - increase_resource_request
forbiddenActions:
  - direct_kubectl_apply
  - direct_kubectl_patch
  - direct_kubectl_delete
  - direct_shell
  - direct_ssh
  - shrink_pvc
  - merge_own_pr

prTarget:
  repository: observability-gitops
  pathTemplate: clusters/{cluster}/namespaces/{namespace}/mimir/values.yaml
  field: compactor.persistentVolume.size
  allowedFields:
    - compactor.persistentVolume.size

sampleChange:
  action: increase_pvc_size
  currentValue: 200Gi
  proposedValue: 300Gi

costControls:
  maxIncreasePercent: 50
  maxPvcSize: 1Ti

The catalog is operational knowledge, not executable permission. It defines a bounded hypothesis: for this failure mode, a larger PVC may be proposed through one GitOps field, within cost constraints, subject to evidence, policy, and human review.

4. Kestra execution, request by request

Kestra does not call Claude directly. Every task calls the planner gateway over HTTP with X-AIOPS-Gateway-Token. The gateway owns model access, contracts, policy, state, and provider credentials.

Order Kestra task Gateway route Input added Durable or external effect Timeout / retry
1 collect_evidence POST /v1/evidence Raw alert Server-issued evidence and identity state 30 s; 3 attempts / 5 s
2 planner POST /v1/plan Evidence Server-issued plan state; optional Claude call 30 s; 3 / 5 s
3 policy POST /v1/policy Evidence + plan OPA decision and policy digest 30 s; 3 / 5 s
4 request_approval POST /v1/approval/request Evidence + plan + policy Approval record bound to exact intent 30 s; 3 / 5 s
5 provider_preflight POST /v1/providers/preflight Approved components Provider readiness/admission result 30 s; 3 / 5 s
6 create_gitops_pr POST /v1/gitops/pr Full authorization chain Outbox intent, branch, commit, draft PR 60 s; 8 / 10 s
7 notify_provider_sinks POST /v1/providers/notify PR result ITSM/incident update through routed adapters 60 s; 8 / 10 s
8 verify_argo_readonly POST /v1/argo/verify PR result Read-only Argo Application observation 60 s; 8 / 10 s
9 audit POST /v1/audit All stage results Audit projection and append-only event 15 s; 8 / 10 s

The first three calls are the pre-policy phase. Later calls are conditional: policy denial, pending approval, and preflight denial each terminate through a specific audit path without creating a PR.

Two persistence domains, not one shared bag of state

Kestra and the gateway persist different facts. They can use the same managed Postgres server, but they should have separate database users and schemas.

Persistence domain Schema What is stored Why it exists Is it authoritative for a provider retry?
Kestra runtime kestra_runtime Execution id, task-run state, queue position, retry counters, logs, trigger data, and each HTTP response body under outputs.<task>.body Resume and inspect workflow orchestration No
Gateway control plane aiops_control_plane Server-issued evidence/plan/policy components, versioned identities, attempts, approvals, provider preflight, transactional outbox, compensation, and audit chain Decide whether an external effect is authorized, new, replayed, ambiguous, or complete Yes
External systems of record GitHub, Argo CD, ITSM, incident provider Draft PR, observed Git revision, change/ticket, incident/event Hold provider truth outside either application database Yes, through deterministic readback

Kestra temporarily carries the component chain because the next task needs it. For example, the plan request embeds the exact body returned by collect_evidence. That copy is useful execution history, but the gateway does not trust it merely because Kestra sent it back. Before a side effect, the gateway reloads server-issued approval and operation state from aiops_control_plane, recomputes the bindings, and rejects a changed component.

ImportantWhat survives which failure

If a Kestra pod restarts, Kestra resumes from kestra_runtime. If a gateway pod restarts, another gateway replica resumes from aiops_control_plane. If the gateway loses a GitHub response, it combines the outbox row with GitHub readback. No one database claims to be a distributed transaction across all three systems.

The request accumulator

Every successful task returns one typed JSON component. Kestra stores that body in its execution state and embeds it unchanged in later requests. The body grows as an authorization chain; Kestra does not manufacture policy or authority.

1 Evidenceraw alert becomes evidence
2 Planevidence becomes plan
3 Policyevidence + plan become policy
4 Approvaladds server-issued approval
5 Preflightadds providerPreflight
6 GitOpsadds gitopsPr
7 Notifyadds provider results
8 Verifyadds argoVerification
9 Auditreconstructs durable truth
After task Kestra runtime output now available Gateway durable write Important authority distinction
1 outputs.collect_evidence.body aiops_state_records(evidence, auditCaseKey) Gateway issued the normalized component
2 outputs.planner.body aiops_state_records(plans, auditCaseKey) Model proposed; it did not authorize
3 outputs.policy.body aiops_state_records(policies, auditCaseKey) OPA allowed or denied the exact plan
4 outputs.request_approval.body aiops_remediation_attempts(attemptKey) and aiops_state_records(approvals, attemptKey) Approval binds component hashes and expiry
5 outputs.provider_preflight.body aiops_state_records(provider_preflight, attemptKey) Availability check is not a provider write
6 outputs.create_gitops_pr.body aiops_outbox_records(gitops operationKey) Durable intent exists before GitHub create
7 outputs.notify_provider_sinks.body One outbox row per provider operation key Each provider has an independent replay scope
8 outputs.verify_argo_readonly.body aiops_outbox_records(argo_verify operationKey) Read-only verification is revision-bound
9 outputs.audit.body aiops_audit_records + aiops_audit_events Latest projection plus append-only history

The remainder of this section follows that accumulator one HTTP exchange at a time. Each blue REQUEST label is what Kestra sends. Each green RESPONSE label is what the gateway returns and Kestra stores. Each amber PERSISTENCE label identifies the gateway-owned durable state.

4.0 Inside every gateway POST

The FastAPI boundary performs the same envelope processing for each route:

Order Executing component Operation Failure effect
1 FastAPI route Parse the route-specific Pydantic request model Reject malformed or unknown fields before workflow code
2 Gateway authentication dependency Validate X-AIOPS-Gateway-Token and caller scope Return 401 or scoped authorization failure
3 Request guard Enforce body limit, route, catalog, and topology scope Reject oversized or cross-scope requests
4 OpenTelemetry middleware Continue inbound trace and attach bounded attributes Preserve correlation without recording raw sensitive payloads
5 Bounded worker pool Run synchronous workflow/provider operation outside the event loop Limit concurrency; protect FastAPI’s event loop from blocking SDK calls
6 Route response model Validate the operation result against Pydantic Prevent an untyped provider response from becoming a workflow contract
7 Telemetry boundary Record route, status, duration, and safe response shape Support diagnosis without emitting credentials or raw provider bodies

The offload matters because GitHub, Postgres, Kubernetes, OPA, and model calls are blocking operations. They are not executed directly on FastAPI’s asyncio event loop. Readiness and metrics use a separate probe pool so a slow provider call does not prevent Kubernetes from checking the pod.

HTTP result Meaning to Kestra
200 Contract-valid stage result; inspect decision, approved, or created rather than assuming authorization
400 Malformed or contract-invalid request; retrying unchanged input is not useful
401 Missing or invalid gateway caller credential
409 Identity, approval, binding, or state-transition conflict
413 Request exceeded the configured body limit
422 Semantically rejected request where exposed by the route contract
500 / 502 / 503 Gateway, downstream provider, or state backend failure; Kestra applies the route-specific retry budget

4.1 Evidence

1

Normalize the external event

Kestra sends the Grafana body; the gateway selects the catalog, normalizes topology, redacts unsafe fields, derives alert-level identity, and issues the evidence component.

Request Gateway persistence: evidence component

Kestra wraps a standard Alertmanager body under alert before calling:

POST /v1/evidence
X-AIOPS-Gateway-Token: <internal-shared-secret>
Content-Type: application/json
{
  "alert": {
    "version": "4",
    "status": "firing",
    "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
    "alerts": [
      {
        "status": "firing",
        "fingerprint": "grafana-labels-fingerprint-9d8c7b6a",
        "labels": {
          "alertname": "MimirCompactorPVCPressure",
          "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
          "cell_id": "contextual-cell-a",
          "namespace": "mimir",
          "workload": "mimir-compactor",
          "persistentvolumeclaim": "mimir-compactor-data",
          "severity": "warning"
        },
        "annotations": {
          "summary": "Mimir compactor PVC pressure",
          "description": "Mimir compactor PVC has less than 15 percent free capacity."
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}

Response

The response is a server-issued EvidenceComponent. It contains normalized topology, redaction metadata, evidence completeness, selected metrics, and the authoritative current GitOps value when available. Later stages should use this component instead of carrying the raw Grafana payload.

Representative contract-valid body for this execution:

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:00.000Z",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
  "cellId": "contextual-cell-a",
  "namespace": "mimir",
  "workload": "mimir-compactor",
  "sensitiveDataIncluded": false,
  "redaction": {
    "applied": true,
    "removedFields": ["generatorURL", "externalURL"],
    "sensitiveDataDetected": false
  },
  "evidenceCompleteness": {
    "schemaVersion": "aiops.evidence-completeness.v1",
    "status": "complete",
    "enforced": true,
    "requiredSignals": ["lab_fixture"],
    "observedSignals": ["lab_fixture", "gitops_current_value"],
    "observedRequiredSignals": ["lab_fixture"],
    "missingRequiredSignals": [],
    "requiredCount": 1,
    "observedRequiredCount": 1,
    "missingRequiredCount": 0
  },
  "syntheticMetrics": false,
  "gitopsAuthoritativeCurrent": {
    "source": "github-base-branch",
    "repository": "example-org/observability-gitops",
    "baseBranch": "main",
    "targetPath": "clusters/contextual-cellgroup-a/namespaces/mimir/mimir/values.yaml",
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "value": "200Gi"
  },
  "identity": {
    "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1",
    "eventKey": "alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794",
    "eventKeyKind": "native",
    "resourceScopeKey": "remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "alertFingerprint": "alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794",
    "alertFingerprintKind": "native",
    "alertCount": 1,
    "topologyScope": {
      "cellGroup": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
      "cell": "contextual-cell-a",
      "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
      "namespace": "mimir",
      "workload": "mimir-compactor",
      "resource": "mimir-compactor-data"
    },
    "topologyAmbiguous": false,
    "topologyVariantCount": 1,
    "remediationScopeKey": "remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4",
    "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "requiredTopology": ["cellGroup", "cluster", "namespace", "resource"],
    "identityTopology": ["cellGroup", "cluster", "namespace", "resource"],
    "identityTopologyScope": {
      "cellGroup": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
      "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
      "namespace": "mimir",
      "resource": "mimir-compactor-data"
    },
    "scopeLabelNames": [],
    "scopeLabelValues": {},
    "missingRequiredTopology": []
  }
}
Read this as: Kestra now owns a workflow copy at outputs.collect_evidence.body. The gateway owns the authoritative copy at aiops_control_plane.aiops_state_records with category=‘evidence’ and idempotency_key=‘audit:v1:9c3cff…’. The identity object is issued here so later stages do not need the raw alert.

4.2 Identity derivation

The Grafana fingerprint is an input, not the final database key. The gateway derives separate identities for different questions:

Identity Question answered Stable inputs Exact value for this trace
Alert fingerprint Which source alert episode arrived? Native Grafana fingerprint(s) alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794
Remediation scope key Which catalog target and topology are affected? Catalog meaning, target, cell group, cluster, namespace, resource remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4
Case key Which recurring operational case is this? Failure mode, target, topology case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3
Audit case key Which alert episode affected which remediation scope? Alert fingerprint + remediation scope audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b
Change-intent hash What exact bounded change is proposed? Stable evidence + plan + policy + catalog digests change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8
Attempt key Which remediation attempt is this? Remediation scope + change intent attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf
Operation key Which one GitHub PR effect is being attempted? Attempt key + gitops_pr side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6

The one canonical hashing rule

All identities in this section use the same deterministic primitive:

canonical = json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), allow_nan=False)
full_digest = hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
public_key = f"{namespace}:v1:{full_digest[:32]}"
Rule Consequence
Only JSON-native values are accepted No Python object representation, set ordering, or class name can leak into identity
Object keys are sorted Input field order cannot change a hash
Separators contain no spaces The same semantic object has one byte representation
NaN and infinity are forbidden Platform-specific non-finite encodings cannot enter identity
UTF-8 is explicit The input byte sequence is deterministic
SHA-256 is calculated in full Each derivation has a 64-hex-character, 256-bit digest
Public keys retain the first 32 hex characters Versioned operational keys expose 128 bits and remain practical in indexes and logs
The namespace and v1 prefix are outside the hash Humans can distinguish semantics and future versions can coexist
NoteHow to read the JSON below

The JSON values are indented for people. Immediately before hashing, the gateway serializes the same value as key-sorted, compact UTF-8 JSON using the function above. Display indentation and line breaks are therefore not hash input; field names, values, array order, and object structure are.

The yellow segment below is the 128-bit value retained in the public alert key; the gray segment is still part of the computed SHA-256 but is not copied into that public key.

alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794227f7920962ca585c5e058100f840415

Identity 1: source alert episode

For the single alert in this example, the source value is normalized as:

native:grafana-labels-fingerprint-9d8c7b6a

Even for one alert, the function hashes a sorted JSON array so the same function also handles a multi-alert notification group.

Canonical JSON value (reader-formatted)

[
  "native:grafana-labels-fingerprint-9d8c7b6a"
]

Full SHA-256

2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794227f7920962ca585c5e058100f840415

Public key

alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794
Where this value lives Exact location
Evidence returned to Kestra EvidenceComponent.identity.alertFingerprint
Gateway state copy aiops_state_records.document -> identity ->> alertFingerprint
Attempt forensic context aiops_remediation_attempts.document ->> alertFingerprint
Audit linkage aiops_audit_records.document -> identityContext ->> alertFingerprint

The raw Grafana fingerprint is not used directly as an outbox key. It identifies one source episode; it says nothing about which catalog target, proposed change, approval, or external operation is involved.

Identity input 2: material catalog digest

The catalog file contains titles, owners, tests, and other useful metadata. Only fields that can change remediation meaning enter catalog_identity_digest.

Canonical catalog material and full digest 
{
  "allowedPrActions": [
    "update_helm_value",
    "increase_pvc_size",
    "increase_resource_request"
  ],
  "component": "mimir-compactor",
  "costControls": {
    "maxIncreasePercent": 50,
    "maxPvcSize": "1Ti"
  },
  "forbiddenActions": [
    "direct_kubectl_apply",
    "direct_kubectl_patch",
    "direct_kubectl_delete",
    "direct_shell",
    "direct_ssh",
    "disable_alert",
    "reduce_retention",
    "shrink_pvc",
    "merge_own_pr"
  ],
  "id": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "identityTopology": [
    "cellGroup",
    "cluster",
    "namespace",
    "resource"
  ],
  "mutationBoundary": "gitops_pull_request",
  "prTarget": {
    "allowedFields": [
      "compactor.persistentVolume.size"
    ],
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "pathTemplate": "clusters/{cluster}/namespaces/{namespace}/mimir/values.yaml",
    "repository": "observability-gitops"
  },
  "requiredTopology": [
    "cellGroup",
    "cluster",
    "namespace",
    "resource"
  ],
  "statefulness": "stateful",
  "verification": [
    "kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes / kubelet_volume_stats_capacity_bytes < 0.75",
    "time() - mimir_compactor_last_successful_run_timestamp_seconds < 1800",
    "argocd: Synced",
    "argocd: Healthy"
  ]
}
55f9244ae2a08161329e103347835f8b5ad0991c2beae7335e40efcbc2513f37

This full 256-bit catalog digest is embedded in the remediation-scope preimage, the change-intent preimage, and the approval binding as catalogHash.

Identity 2: remediation scope

This key answers “which governed target under which topology and catalog meaning?” It combines the material catalog digest, the allowed GitOps target, the selected identity topology, and any catalog-defined scope labels.

Canonical JSON value (reader-formatted)

{
  "catalogDigest": "55f9244ae2a08161329e103347835f8b5ad0991c2beae7335e40efcbc2513f37",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1",
  "scopeLabels": {},
  "target": {
    "allowedFields": [
      "compactor.persistentVolume.size"
    ],
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "pathTemplate": "clusters/{cluster}/namespaces/{namespace}/mimir/values.yaml",
    "repository": "observability-gitops",
    "resource": "mimir-compactor"
  },
  "topologyScope": {
    "cellGroup": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
    "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
    "namespace": "mimir",
    "resource": "mimir-compactor-data"
  }
}

Full SHA-256

ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4ac00b1ea574af200d2215a785ff41147

Public key

remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4

The cell value is present in the full evidence topology but is deliberately absent from this catalog’s identityTopology. Here, the cell group/cluster, namespace, and PVC resource define the remediation scope. A catalog can include cell when a node-group-level distinction changes the target.

Identity 3: recurring operational case

The case key is related to, but not identical to, the remediation-scope key. It uses failureModeKey, target, topology, and scope labels, but does not include the catalog digest. This lets the same recurring operational case remain grouped across non-structural catalog revisions.

Canonical JSON value (reader-formatted)

{
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "failureModeKey": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1",
  "scopeLabels": {},
  "target": {
    "allowedFields": [
      "compactor.persistentVolume.size"
    ],
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "pathTemplate": "clusters/{cluster}/namespaces/{namespace}/mimir/values.yaml",
    "repository": "observability-gitops",
    "resource": "mimir-compactor"
  },
  "topologyScope": {
    "cellGroup": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
    "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
    "namespace": "mimir",
    "resource": "mimir-compactor-data"
  }
}

Full SHA-256 and public key

6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d33e00c0e25766c477fcf74fdc2f18e800
case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3

It is stored as the indexed case_key column in aiops_remediation_attempts, then repeated inside identity/audit JSON for forensic reconstruction.

Identity 4: alert-to-scope audit case

The audit case is a composite identity: source episode plus governed remediation scope. A second Grafana episode for the same target gets a different audit case, while the target scope remains recognizable.

Canonical JSON value (reader-formatted)

{
  "alertFingerprint": "alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794",
  "remediationScopeKey": "remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1"
}

Full SHA-256 and public key

9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b68de613c161f4825c4c7eeef418188c5
audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b

Before an attempt exists, this key is the durable lookup key for evidence, plan, and policy rows in aiops_state_records. It is also an indexed column in the attempt and audit tables.

Stable component digests used by the change intent

The change intent is not a hash of the full HTTP bodies. Each component is first projected to fields that materially define authorization.

Component Included Deliberately excluded
Evidence catalogEntryId, cellId, cluster, namespace, workload Timestamp, live metrics, redaction notes, free-form source metadata
Plan catalogEntryId, decision, canonical proposedChange, verification Timestamp, model/provider name, confidence, rationale wording
Policy Decision, sorted violations, boundary, sorted forbidden actions, kill switch Timestamp, policy engine label, OPA bundle digest
Catalog Material identity projection shown above Title, owner, tests, descriptive metadata

Evidence digest

{
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "cellId": "contextual-cell-a",
  "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
  "namespace": "mimir",
  "workload": "mimir-compactor"
}
54b4a002e09c8e359888d3e8e7a675d5d6ef31f73ecd36dac997741dddd3a052

Plan digest

Before hashing, Kubernetes quantities are converted to semantic forms. Therefore 200Gi, 0200Gi, and another accepted representation of the same binary quantity do not create different intent merely because their strings differ.

{
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "decision": "propose_gitops_pr",
  "proposedChange": {
    "action": "increase_pvc_size",
    "currentValue": {
      "kind": "binary_quantity_gi",
      "value": "2E+2"
    },
    "directMutation": false,
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "proposedValue": {
      "kind": "binary_quantity_gi",
      "value": "3E+2"
    },
    "targetPath": "clusters/contextual-cellgroup-a/namespaces/mimir/mimir/values.yaml",
    "targetRepository": "observability-gitops"
  },
  "verification": [
    "PVC utilization falls below 75 percent",
    "Mimir compactor succeeds within 30 minutes",
    "Argo CD reports Synced and Healthy"
  ]
}
dcc3b25ae14755acea99e78d8629c9346e9e7f39bc86824178113c81409aea0f

Stable policy-binding digest

{
  "decision": "allow",
  "enforcedBoundary": "gitops_pull_request",
  "forbiddenActions": [
    "direct_kubectl_apply",
    "direct_kubectl_delete",
    "direct_kubectl_patch",
    "direct_shell",
    "direct_ssh",
    "disable_alert",
    "merge_own_pr",
    "reduce_retention",
    "shrink_pvc"
  ],
  "killSwitchEnabled": false,
  "violations": []
}
c9dc8589eb122a07c16f5699fc3ed5df92e13fa7b7bc2421b4f18593606cf875
NoteTwo policy hashes with different purposes

policyBundleDigest is SHA-256 of the loaded Rego bundle bytes. It supports provenance and cache invalidation. policyHash is the semantic digest above and is used in approval/change identity. In the current implementation, changing Rego bytes without changing the material decision does not create a new change intent. The bundle digest remains in the policy component and audit record.

Identity 5: exact change intent

The change-intent preimage is a composition of four full component digests. It does not hash a model’s prose and does not trust display formatting.

Canonical JSON value (reader-formatted)

{
  "catalogDigest": "55f9244ae2a08161329e103347835f8b5ad0991c2beae7335e40efcbc2513f37",
  "evidenceDigest": "54b4a002e09c8e359888d3e8e7a675d5d6ef31f73ecd36dac997741dddd3a052",
  "planDigest": "dcc3b25ae14755acea99e78d8629c9346e9e7f39bc86824178113c81409aea0f",
  "policyDigest": "c9dc8589eb122a07c16f5699fc3ed5df92e13fa7b7bc2421b4f18593606cf875",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1"
}

Full SHA-256 and public key

724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad80d5d18d64cd8459dd08487092585275c
change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8

Identity 6: remediation attempt

The attempt is another composite identity. It joins the exact bounded change to the governed remediation scope. It deliberately does not include the source alert fingerprint. If two alert episodes ask for the same already-approved change on the same scope, they converge on one attempt and one PR while retaining separate audit-case lineage.

{
  "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
  "remediationScopeKey": "remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1"
}
c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf01172ffdfae0ac393cc77b5798d1ed64
attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf

This is the primary key of aiops_remediation_attempts, the lookup key for the approval/preflight components, and the generated foreign-key spine in outbox, audit, and compensation records.

Identity 7: one key per external operation

Each external effect gets a separate operation key. The operation name prevents a GitHub retry from colliding with an ITSM, incident, Argo, or audit operation.

{
  "attemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
  "operation": "gitops_pr",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1"
}
Operation name in preimage Full SHA-256 Public operation key
gitops_pr e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae618c52e3168c08be3f01befc4ffe18e82 side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6
servicenow 8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a679d7e31b21831c9b7b70028069745af89 side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67
pagerduty 4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c7ffdab57657517ecb4ece98f346862cf side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c
argo_verify 1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a6794f4133578661fc167b016d2c68549 side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a
audit_bundle 12473c4dca6b30d5a32263bfd095119c4b3ec1033bbfb57ca9c33af3b130db76 side-effect:v1:12473c4dca6b30d5a32263bfd095119c

Every value above becomes aiops_outbox_records.record_key and operation_key. attempt_key is generated from the JSON identity context and foreign-keyed to aiops_remediation_attempts.

Composite identity and table map

Layer Built from Used as Durable table/column
Alert Sorted normalized native/synthetic source fingerprints Episode correlation JSON in aiops_state_records.document; later audit JSON
Remediation scope Catalog digest + target + identity topology + scope labels Target authorization scope JSON in evidence/attempt/audit identity context
Case Failure mode + target + identity topology + scope labels Recurring operational grouping aiops_remediation_attempts.case_key
Audit case Alert fingerprint + remediation scope Pre-attempt component key and episode lineage aiops_state_records.idempotency_key; aiops_remediation_attempts.audit_case_key; generated audit column
Change intent Evidence + plan + policy + catalog digests Exact bounded proposal Approval binding and attempt JSON
Attempt Remediation scope + change intent Approval and side-effect parent aiops_remediation_attempts.attempt_key; generated foreign keys
Operation Attempt + operation name One independent provider effect aiops_outbox_records.record_key and generated operation_key

Free-form model rationale, live metric fluctuations, and generated timestamps do not re-key the same change. The target field, semantically normalized value, policy result, catalog meaning, and topology do.

This separation prevents two common mistakes:

  1. Treating a Grafana fingerprint as sufficient identity for every side effect.
  2. Creating a second PR because the model worded the same rationale differently.

4.3 Plan

2

Ask Claude for a bounded proposal

Kestra sends only the gateway-issued evidence. The gateway adds catalog context, calls the configured model provider, validates the JSON contract, and persists the typed plan.

Request Gateway persistence: plan component

Kestra sends only the evidence component:

{
  "evidence": {
    "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
    "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
    "cellId": "contextual-cell-a",
    "namespace": "mimir",
    "workload": "mimir-compactor",
    "sensitiveDataIncluded": false,
    "gitopsAuthoritativeCurrent": {
      "repository": "example-org/observability-gitops",
      "baseBranch": "main",
      "targetPath": "clusters/contextual-cellgroup-a/namespaces/mimir/mimir/values.yaml",
      "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
      "value": "200Gi"
    },
    "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b"
  }
}

The literal Kestra source is important because it shows data provenance:

body: |
  {
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

outputs.collect_evidence.body is the complete green response from step 1, stored in the Kestra execution database. Kestra does not select individual evidence fields and does not call the model itself.

The gateway constructs a bounded model prompt from the evidence and catalog. Claude returns a typed proposal; the gateway validates it as PlanComponent. The expected material result is:

Response

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:01.000Z",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "llmUsed": true,
  "decision": "propose_gitops_pr",
  "confidence": "medium",
  "rationale": "Evidence matches the cataloged Mimir compactor PVC-pressure condition. Propose only the allowlisted GitOps size increase; do not mutate Kubernetes directly.",
  "proposedChange": {
    "action": "increase_pvc_size",
    "targetRepository": "observability-gitops",
    "targetPath": "clusters/contextual-cellgroup-a/namespaces/mimir/mimir/values.yaml",
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "currentValue": "200Gi",
    "proposedValue": "300Gi",
    "directMutation": false
  },
  "verification": [
    "PVC utilization falls below 75 percent",
    "Mimir compactor succeeds within 30 minutes",
    "Argo CD reports Synced and Healthy"
  ]
}
Read this as: Kestra stores this body at outputs.planner.body. The gateway stores it at aiops_state_records(category=‘plans’, idempotency_key=‘audit:v1:9c3cff…’). The model’s rationale and confidence remain audit evidence, but the stable plan digest excludes them so a wording change cannot create a second PR.

The model does not receive GitHub, Kubernetes, ServiceNow, PagerDuty, or Argo credentials. A schema-valid response is still only a proposal; policy evaluates its semantics in the next call.

4.4 Policy

3

Evaluate the proposal at the OPA boundary

Kestra sends the two prior gateway responses. The gateway rebuilds policy input from typed components and the catalog, calls OPA, and persists the semantic decision plus bundle provenance.

Request Gateway persistence: policy component

Kestra posts evidence and plan to /v1/policy. OPA evaluates a policy input assembled by the gateway. Important checks include:

body: |
  {
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "plan": {{ outputs.planner.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

On the wire, each template expression expands to the complete JSON response already printed above. The request contract has extra="forbid"; an unknown top-level control field is rejected before policy code runs.

Check Expected result for this example
Runtime kill switch Disabled
Planner decision propose_gitops_pr
Requested action Present in catalog allowedPrActions
Direct mutation Exactly false
Repository and target path Match catalog target
Field compactor.persistentVolume.size only
Current and proposed value Different
PVC direction Increase, never shrink
Increase percentage At or below 50%
Maximum size At or below 1Ti
Required topology Present and unambiguous
Sensitive content Not present in proposed change

Response

Allowed response:

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:02.000Z",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "decision": "allow",
  "violations": [],
  "policyEngine": "opa",
  "policyBundleDigest": "sha256:2494fdc59fc9eadd89b59bd387a04c12ef91c69bee9af384dbde1d6697f5b8a6",
  "enforcedBoundary": "gitops_pull_request",
  "forbiddenActions": [
    "direct_kubectl_apply",
    "direct_kubectl_patch",
    "direct_kubectl_delete",
    "direct_shell",
    "direct_ssh",
    "disable_alert",
    "reduce_retention",
    "shrink_pvc",
    "merge_own_pr"
  ],
  "killSwitchEnabled": false
}

OPA does not create a PR. It returns a decision that becomes one input to the authorization binding.

Read this as: Kestra now has outputs.policy.body. The gateway has aiops_state_records(category=‘policies’, idempotency_key=‘audit:v1:9c3cff…’). The displayed policyBundleDigest identifies policy bytes in that runtime; the stable approval policyHash is the separately documented semantic digest c9dc8589….

4.5 Approval

4

Issue approval for one immutable intent

Kestra sends evidence, plan, and policy. The gateway recomputes every digest, creates the attempt spine, and returns an approval record whose scope cannot be reused for another target or value.

Request Gateway persistence: attempt + approval

The exact Kestra template is:

body: |
  {
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "plan": {{ outputs.planner.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "policy": {{ outputs.policy.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

At this point, the request contains three complete server-issued components. The gateway validates them, recomputes the hashes printed in section 4.2, and creates the following binding:

{
  "alertFingerprint": "alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794",
  "planHash": "dcc3b25ae14755acea99e78d8629c9346e9e7f39bc86824178113c81409aea0f",
  "policyHash": "c9dc8589eb122a07c16f5699fc3ed5df92e13fa7b7bc2421b4f18593606cf875",
  "evidenceHash": "54b4a002e09c8e359888d3e8e7a675d5d6ef31f73ecd36dac997741dddd3a052",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "catalogHash": "55f9244ae2a08161329e103347835f8b5ad0991c2beae7335e40efcbc2513f37",
  "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
  "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
  "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
  "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"
}

The approval endpoint does not accept a free-floating Boolean. The gateway issues an approval record for the exact attempt and stores it in Postgres. Its binding includes:

Binding field Protects against
caseKey Applying approval to another operational case
catalogEntryId and catalogHash Catalog changed after review
evidenceHash Evidence substituted after review
planHash Plan changed after review
policyHash Policy result changed after review
changeIntentHash Target, field, or value changed after review
remediationAttemptId Approval replayed across attempts
idempotencyKey Approval replayed across the wrong durable operation
Expiry and evidence age Stale authorization

Response

For a fully exercised sandbox path using mock-approved, the response has this shape. The approval id below is reproducible from the binding, mode, and shown issue/expiry epochs; a live run naturally has different timestamp-derived id.

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:03.000Z",
  "approvalId": "c4d29e50dc3cbe94d29052bc",
  "requested": true,
  "approved": true,
  "mode": "mock-approved",
  "reason": "approved_for_poc",
  "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
  "identityContext": {
    "issuedComponentKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "approvalAttemptKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "attemptKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "alertFingerprint": "alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794",
    "resourceScopeKey": "remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
    "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"
  },
  "approvalBinding": {
    "alertFingerprint": "alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794",
    "planHash": "dcc3b25ae14755acea99e78d8629c9346e9e7f39bc86824178113c81409aea0f",
    "policyHash": "c9dc8589eb122a07c16f5699fc3ed5df92e13fa7b7bc2421b4f18593606cf875",
    "evidenceHash": "54b4a002e09c8e359888d3e8e7a675d5d6ef31f73ecd36dac997741dddd3a052",
    "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
    "catalogHash": "55f9244ae2a08161329e103347835f8b5ad0991c2beae7335e40efcbc2513f37",
    "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
    "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"
  },
  "issuedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:03.000Z",
  "issuedAtEpoch": 1783872003,
  "expiresAt": "2026-07-12T17:00:03.000Z",
  "expiresAtEpoch": 1783875603,
  "maxAgeSeconds": 3600,
  "evidenceGeneratedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:00.000Z",
  "evidenceGeneratedAtEpoch": 1783872000,
  "evidenceAgeAtIssueSeconds": 3,
  "maxEvidenceAgeAtApprovalSeconds": 600,
  "freshnessViolations": [],
  "approverGroups": ["observability-platform-approvers"],
  "changeWindowRequired": true,
  "serviceNowCorrelationId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"
}
Read this as: Kestra has a copy at outputs.request_approval.body. The gateway creates aiops_remediation_attempts.attempt_key=‘attempt:v1:c6a…’ and aiops_state_records(category=‘approvals’, idempotency_key=‘attempt:v1:c6a…’). Side-effect code later reloads this row; changing the Boolean in Kestra’s copy is insufficient.

In manual mode, the first call returns a pending approval. A separate approved identity calls /v1/approval/grant with approve or deny. A committed manual decision is terminal: an identical retry replays the decision; an attempted reversal is rejected.

NoteLab evidence versus production

The 29-case lab used mock-approved to exercise the complete binding contract without an enterprise approval system. Production should use manual or enterprise approval while preserving the same server-issued binding. The lab mode is evidence of contract behavior, not evidence that approval can be skipped.

4.6 Provider preflight

5

Prove the selected routes are admissible and reachable

Kestra adds the approval. The gateway resolves the governed sink-routing table, asks OPA to admit those routes, checks provider connectivity, and binds the result to the same attempt.

Request Gateway persistence: provider preflight

This first block is Kestra YAML, not the final wire body. The YAML body scalar contains a JSON template. Kestra evaluates each expression, inserts the complete JSON component returned by the earlier task, and sends the resulting body with Content-Type: application/json.

body: |
  {
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "plan": {{ outputs.planner.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "policy": {{ outputs.policy.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "approval": {{ outputs.request_approval.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

Preflight verifies that the selected provider routes and credentials are ready before any external write. It also binds the provider decision to the same attempt identity. A preflight denial produces an audited terminal result and no GitHub, ITSM, or incident side effect.

Response

The following is a complete representative sandbox response for HTTP-backed ServiceNow and PagerDuty simulators. In an enterprise environment, the same contract carries the approved real provider modes.

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:04.000Z",
  "preflightId": "f5eeedb2eedced7c7a43291a",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
  "decision": "allow",
  "identityContext": {
    "issuedComponentKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "attemptKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
    "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"
  },
  "sinkRouting": {
    "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
    "policy": "default-remediation-v1",
    "matchedRuleId": "remediation-default",
    "routingTableDigest": "249656c8a2565f8a456ca1e8117a107772449f9cef5b1cf73448a6dbe06ede08",
    "routingContext": {
      "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
      "environment": "lab",
      "severity": "unknown",
      "cellId": "contextual-cell-a"
    },
    "routes": [
      {"role": "itsm", "mode": "http", "provider": "servicenow", "reason": "default-remediation-change-context", "required": true},
      {"role": "incident", "mode": "http", "provider": "pagerduty", "reason": "default-remediation-incident-context", "required": true}
    ]
  },
  "sinkRouteAdmission": {
    "schemaVersion": "aiops.sink-route-admission.v1",
    "decision": "allow",
    "violations": []
  },
  "checks": [
    {
      "provider": "servicenow",
      "role": "itsm",
      "mode": "http",
      "required": true,
      "routingReason": "default-remediation-change-context",
      "status": "passed",
      "sideEffectCreated": false
    },
    {
      "provider": "pagerduty",
      "role": "incident",
      "mode": "http",
      "required": true,
      "routingReason": "default-remediation-incident-context",
      "status": "passed",
      "sideEffectCreated": false
    }
  ],
  "providerPreflightBinding": {
    "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
    "catalogHash": "55f9244ae2a08161329e103347835f8b5ad0991c2beae7335e40efcbc2513f37",
    "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
    "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "sinkRoutingPolicy": "default-remediation-v1",
    "sinkRoutingHash": "b0c82e3f85159269dae3a14456a96cc0341c64b133772cc9afd0f7ca0bd47f15",
    "sinkRouteAdmissionHash": "00a8ef38bae125b628a50f40ff9a9cbd6049a87a79e9d0358e2eb3f4435db5e0",
    "providerModes": {"servicenow": "http", "pagerduty": "http"},
    "routeProviders": ["servicenow", "pagerduty"],
    "checkProviders": ["servicenow", "pagerduty"]
  },
  "violations": [],
  "directMutation": false
}

preflightId is the first 24 hex characters of SHA-256 over {providerPreflightBinding, decision, violations}. It is not an outbox key and preflight creates no provider side effect.

Read this as: Kestra stores this as outputs.provider_preflight.body. The gateway stores the server-issued copy at aiops_state_records(category=‘providerPreflight’, idempotency_key=‘attempt:v1:c6a…’). Before every effect, the gateway recomputes current routing/admission hashes and rejects stale or substituted preflight.

4.7 Draft GitOps PR and the transactional outbox

6

Create or recover one draft pull request

Kestra sends the full authorization chain. The gateway reloads trusted components, records durable intent, claims the operation, reads GitHub by deterministic branch, and creates only if absent.

Request Gateway persistence: GitHub outbox operation

body: |
  {
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "plan": {{ outputs.planner.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "policy": {{ outputs.policy.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "approval": {{ outputs.request_approval.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "providerPreflight": {{ outputs.provider_preflight.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

POST /v1/gitops/pr re-evaluates the trusted policy, loads the server-issued approval from durable state, checks freshness and all binding hashes, and then enters the shared SideEffectExecutor.

Before inserting the outbox row, the gateway computes two additional full SHA-256 guards. They are equality guards, not public identity namespaces.

Guard Exact value What a mismatch means
authorizationBindingHash faec972905216e8bb5c95975b502b8d200221b5e2ce05153fec393ad19cd446f The durable approval binding or proposed change differs from the operation request
requestScopeHash 73d4bc190257f0be90d95903b892ceed2ca184b99f6bbc9da1b27464cfeb0b33 Operation, side-effect type, catalog, orchestrator, attempt scope, or authorization differs
Authorization-binding JSON value (reader-formatted) 
{
  "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "catalogHash": "55f9244ae2a08161329e103347835f8b5ad0991c2beae7335e40efcbc2513f37",
  "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
  "evidenceHash": "54b4a002e09c8e359888d3e8e7a675d5d6ef31f73ecd36dac997741dddd3a052",
  "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
  "planHash": "dcc3b25ae14755acea99e78d8629c9346e9e7f39bc86824178113c81409aea0f",
  "policyHash": "c9dc8589eb122a07c16f5699fc3ed5df92e13fa7b7bc2421b4f18593606cf875",
  "proposedChange": {
    "action": "increase_pvc_size",
    "currentValue": "200Gi",
    "directMutation": false,
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "proposedValue": "300Gi",
    "targetPath": "clusters/contextual-cellgroup-a/namespaces/mimir/mimir/values.yaml",
    "targetRepository": "observability-gitops"
  },
  "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"
}
Outbox request-scope JSON value (reader-formatted) 
{
  "authorizationBindingHash": "faec972905216e8bb5c95975b502b8d200221b5e2ce05153fec393ad19cd446f",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "identityContext": {
    "approvalAttemptKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "attemptKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
    "operation": "gitops_pr",
    "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
    "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "resourceScopeKey": "remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4",
    "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1"
  },
  "operation": "gitops_pr",
  "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
  "orchestrator": "kestra",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.outbox.request-scope.v1",
  "sideEffectType": "git_pull_request"
}

The critical order is fixed: derive the operation key; insert durable intent; atomically claim; read GitHub by deterministic correlation; create only when absent; persist the result; return either the created object or an idempotent replay. The database transition table and concrete row documents below make that order reviewable.

Outbox state transitions

State Meaning Safe retry behavior
intent_recorded The intended effect is durable; no replica owns the provider call yet Competing replicas may attempt to claim
provider_call_claimed One replica owns a bounded lease Other replicas wait, read, or retry after lease expiry
completed Provider object and correlation data are durably recorded Return the same result; do not call provider again
failed Failure occurred before a confirmed provider effect Retry only under bounded policy/backoff
unknown_external_state Provider may have accepted the write but response was lost Read provider by correlation key before any create retry
compensation_required A later stage failed after an earlier external effect Surface recovery work; do not pretend the transaction rolled back

Two replicas, one PR

There is no claim of exactly-once delivery across Postgres and GitHub. The achievable guarantee is: durable intent, stable correlation identity, one active claimant, provider readback after ambiguity, and convergence to one PR.

The operation key also determines the provider correlation:

Derived value Formula Concrete value
Outbox id First 24 hex of SHA-256 over the operation-key string e5961d0476b1577601a96de5
GitHub head branch aiops-<catalog-slug>- + first 12 hex of the same string hash aiops-mimir-compactor-pvc-pressure-e5961d0476b1
Provider side-effect id pr:<GitHub PR number> after readback/create pr:82 in the representative evidence run

Response

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:05.000Z",
  "created": true,
  "directMutation": false,
  "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "provider": "github-api",
  "gitApiFlavor": "github",
  "repository": "example-org/observability-gitops",
  "baseBranch": "main",
  "branch": "aiops-mimir-compactor-pvc-pressure-e5961d0476b1",
  "targetPath": "clusters/contextual-cellgroup-a/namespaces/mimir/mimir/values.yaml",
  "prNumber": 82,
  "url": "https://github.com/example-org/observability-gitops/pull/82",
  "apiUrl": "https://api.github.com/repos/example-org/observability-gitops/pulls/82",
  "sideEffectId": "pr:82",
  "title": "AIOps: propose Mimir compactor PVC pressure remediation",
  "patch": {
    "compactor.persistentVolume.size": {"from": "200Gi", "to": "300Gi"}
  },
  "requiresHumanApproval": true,
  "autoMerge": false,
  "allowsSelfMerge": false,
  "reviewControls": {
    "requiresHumanApproval": true,
    "requiresBranchProtection": true,
    "requiredApproverGroups": ["observability-platform-approvers"],
    "autoMerge": false,
    "allowsSelfMerge": false
  },
  "merged": false,
  "mergeCommitSha": null,
  "expectedArgoRevision": null,
  "outbox": {
    "outboxId": "e5961d0476b1577601a96de5",
    "operation": "gitops_pr",
    "status": "completed",
    "preSideEffectRecorded": true,
    "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
    "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
    "authorizationBindingHash": "faec972905216e8bb5c95975b502b8d200221b5e2ce05153fec393ad19cd446f",
    "requestScopeHash": "73d4bc190257f0be90d95903b892ceed2ca184b99f6bbc9da1b27464cfeb0b33",
    "sideEffectId": "pr:82",
    "latestOutcome": "created",
    "attemptCount": 1,
    "providerAttemptCount": 1,
    "replayCount": 0
  }
}
Read this as: Kestra stores the returned component at outputs.create_gitops_pr.body. The gateway stores the provider component in aiops_state_records(category=‘gitopsPr’, idempotency_key=‘side-effect:v1:e9b…’) and the durable state machine in aiops_outbox_records(record_key=‘side-effect:v1:e9b…’). GitHub stores branch …-e5961d0476b1 and PR 82. Those three copies serve different purposes.

4.8 The exact configuration change

The proposed pull request is a draft and changes one allowlisted YAML field:

 compactor:
   persistentVolume:
-    size: 200Gi
+    size: 300Gi

The gateway does not merge the pull request and does not patch the PVC. The human reviewer evaluates the evidence, cost, storage-class behavior, rollback or expansion constraints, and the generated diff. Normal GitHub branch protection and CODEOWNERS remain in force.

PVC expansion requires special care: a Helm value and StatefulSet volume claim template can change while an already-bound PVC remains at its old size. Argo Synced/Healthy alone is not proof that the filesystem expanded. The post-merge verification must inspect the bound PVC and workload-visible capacity.

4.9 Provider notifications

7

Fan out the governed PR result

Kestra adds the GitHub result. The gateway re-authorizes the request and executes one independent outbox operation for each admitted provider route.

Request Gateway persistence: one outbox row per sink

body: |
  {
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "plan": {{ outputs.planner.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "policy": {{ outputs.policy.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "approval": {{ outputs.request_approval.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "providerPreflight": {{ outputs.provider_preflight.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "gitopsPr": {{ outputs.create_gitops_pr.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

After the PR result exists, /v1/providers/notify routes the same governed context to ITSM and incident adapters. The adapters use operation-specific idempotency keys, not the Grafana fingerprint directly. For example:

side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67  # ServiceNow operation
side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c  # PagerDuty operation

If a provider accepts a write and the HTTP response is lost, the gateway must read back by correlation/deduplication key. It must not assume the write failed and create a second change or incident.

Response

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:06.000Z",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.provider-notification-fanout.v1",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "decision": "allow",
  "created": true,
  "directMutation": false,
  "sinkRouting": {
    "policy": "default-remediation-v1",
    "matchedRuleId": "remediation-default",
    "routes": [
      {"role": "itsm", "provider": "servicenow", "mode": "http", "required": true},
      {"role": "incident", "provider": "pagerduty", "mode": "http", "required": true}
    ]
  },
  "sinkRouteAdmission": {
    "schemaVersion": "aiops.sink-route-admission.v1",
    "decision": "allow",
    "violations": []
  },
  "dispatches": [
    {"role": "itsm", "provider": "servicenow", "operationKey": "side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67", "status": "completed"},
    {"role": "incident", "provider": "pagerduty", "operationKey": "side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c", "status": "completed"}
  ],
  "serviceNow": {
    "created": true,
    "directMutation": false,
    "provider": "servicenow-table-api",
    "operation": "create_or_update_change",
    "changeId": "chg-aiops-c6a3967e",
    "correlationId": "side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67",
    "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67"
  },
  "pagerDuty": {
    "created": true,
    "provider": "pagerduty-events-api-v2",
    "eventAction": "trigger",
    "dedupKey": "side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c",
    "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c",
    "customDetails": {
      "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
      "prNumber": 82,
      "directMutation": false
    }
  },
  "violations": []
}
Read this as: one Kestra task produces one fan-out response, but the gateway does not collapse the providers into one transaction. ServiceNow and PagerDuty have separate operation keys, separate outbox rows, separate provider readbacks, and separate retry outcomes. A completed GitHub PR is not rolled back because an optional later notification fails.

4.10 Argo verification

8

Observe deployment state without mutating it

Kestra sends the same authorized PR context. The gateway reads one allowlisted Argo Application through the Kubernetes SDK and binds health to the expected merged revision.

Request Gateway persistence: Argo observation operation

body: |
  {
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "plan": {{ outputs.planner.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "policy": {{ outputs.policy.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "approval": {{ outputs.request_approval.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "providerPreflight": {{ outputs.provider_preflight.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "gitopsPr": {{ outputs.create_gitops_pr.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

The gateway calls the Kubernetes API through the Kubernetes client to read the Argo Application custom resource. Its contract is read-only:

Response

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:07.000Z",
  "provider": "kubernetes-argo-application-cr",
  "mode": "read_only",
  "created": true,
  "verificationId": "argo:side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a",
  "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a",
  "directMutation": false,
  "application": "aiops-mimir-wave",
  "applicationSelector": "mimir.*",
  "namespace": "argocd",
  "syncStatus": "Synced",
  "healthStatus": "Healthy",
  "revision": "474092ad87bcf3931deec00d191d6bb1a1068670",
  "observedRevision": "474092ad87bcf3931deec00d191d6bb1a1068670",
  "expectedRevision": null,
  "verificationState": "awaiting_pr_merge_revision",
  "verificationPassed": false,
  "reason": "approved_revision_not_available",
  "prObserved": true,
  "postMergeChecks": [
    "kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes / kubelet_volume_stats_capacity_bytes < 0.75",
    "time() - mimir_compactor_last_successful_run_timestamp_seconds < 1800",
    "argocd: Synced",
    "argocd: Healthy"
  ],
  "metricsVerification": {
    "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:07.000Z",
    "provider": "metrics-verifier",
    "mode": "read_only",
    "verificationState": "no_promql_checks",
    "verificationPassed": true,
    "checkCount": 0,
    "checks": []
  },
  "outbox": {
    "operation": "argo_verify",
    "status": "failed",
    "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a",
    "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a",
    "latestOutcome": "read_only_verification_not_yet_green",
    "observationCount": 1
  }
}

Before merge, this proves only that the PR is observed and identifies the expected revision. After merge, verification must bind to the expected commit or revision; reading an unrelated healthy Argo application is not sufficient.

The failed outbox status above does not mean a Kubernetes mutation failed. It means the read-only observation is not terminally green yet because a draft PR has no merged revision. A later retry re-reads Argo; once expected revision, Synced, Healthy, and metrics checks all agree, the same operation row reaches completed.

Read this as: Kestra stores the observation at outputs.verify_argo_readonly.body. The gateway stores it at aiops_state_records(category=‘argoVerify’, idempotency_key=‘side-effect:v1:1de…’) and tracks retry state in the matching outbox row. The Kubernetes service account has get/list/watch access only to selected Argo Application resources.

4.11 Audit

9

Reconstruct and seal the execution record

Kestra submits its complete view. The gateway ignores caller claims as authority, reloads each server-issued component and outbox row, checks completeness, writes a latest projection, and appends a tamper-evident event.

Request Gateway persistence: audit projection + event

The exact success-path Kestra template is:

body: |
  {
    "orchestrator": "kestra",
    "flow": "aiops.kestra/catalog_remediation_aiops",
    "evidence": {{ outputs.collect_evidence.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "plan": {{ outputs.planner.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "policy": {{ outputs.policy.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "approval": {{ outputs.request_approval.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "providerPreflight": {{ outputs.provider_preflight.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "gitopsPr": {{ outputs.create_gitops_pr.body | jq('.') | first }},
    "serviceNow": {{ outputs.notify_provider_sinks.body | jq('.serviceNow') | first }},
    "pagerDuty": {{ outputs.notify_provider_sinks.body | jq('.pagerDuty') | first }},
    "argoVerification": {{ outputs.verify_argo_readonly.body | jq('.') | first }}
  }

The caller supplies all stage outputs so the request is self-describing, but the audit implementation reconstructs these component names from gateway state:

Audit field Trusted lookup performed by gateway
evidence, plan, policy Server-issued component rows under the audit case key
approval aiops_state_records('approvals', attemptKey)
providerPreflight aiops_state_records('providerPreflight', attemptKey)
gitopsPr aiops_state_records('gitopsPr', gitops operationKey)
serviceNow aiops_state_records('serviceNow', ServiceNow operationKey)
pagerDuty aiops_state_records('pagerDuty', PagerDuty operationKey)
argoVerification aiops_state_records('argoVerify', Argo operationKey)
Side-effect status All matching aiops_outbox_records for the audit case/attempt

The final audit request carries the stage outputs, but the audit service does not blindly trust caller-provided success fields. It reconstructs trusted state from server-issued components and durable operation records, validates completeness, writes the latest audit projection, and appends a hash-chained audit event.

Response

The response repeats the trusted full components. The excerpt below keeps those already-printed objects compact while showing every top-level authority and linkage field; on the wire, evidence, plan, policy, approval, providerPreflight, and provider results are complete JSON objects.

{
  "generatedAt": "2026-07-12T16:00:08.000Z",
  "orchestrator": "kestra",
  "flow": "aiops.kestra/catalog_remediation_aiops",
  "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:12473c4dca6b30d5a32263bfd095119c",
  "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
  "identityContext": {
    "attemptKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
    "operation": "audit_bundle",
    "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:12473c4dca6b30d5a32263bfd095119c"
  },
  "llmUsed": true,
  "llmProvider": "anthropic",
  "llmModel": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "mutationBoundary": "gitops_pull_request",
  "auditStatus": "complete",
  "auditCompletenessViolations": [],
  "sourceTriggerRetention": {
    "rawPayloadRetained": false,
    "reason": "raw_trigger_not_persisted",
    "retainedDerivedArtifacts": ["evidence", "identityContext"]
  },
  "evidence": {"catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure", "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b"},
  "plan": {"decision": "propose_gitops_pr", "proposedChange": {"field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size", "currentValue": "200Gi", "proposedValue": "300Gi", "directMutation": false}},
  "policy": {"decision": "allow", "violations": [], "enforcedBoundary": "gitops_pull_request"},
  "approval": {"approved": true, "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"},
  "providerPreflight": {"decision": "allow", "preflightId": "f5eeedb2eedced7c7a43291a"},
  "gitopsPr": {"prNumber": 82, "sideEffectId": "pr:82", "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6"},
  "serviceNow": {"correlationId": "side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67"},
  "pagerDuty": {"dedupKey": "side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c"},
  "argoVerification": {"verificationId": "argo:side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a", "verificationState": "awaiting_pr_merge_revision"},
  "verification": [
    "kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes / kubelet_volume_stats_capacity_bytes < 0.75",
    "time() - mimir_compactor_last_successful_run_timestamp_seconds < 1800",
    "argocd: Synced",
    "argocd: Healthy"
  ],
  "sideEffectOutbox": [
    {"operation": "gitops_pr", "status": "completed", "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6"},
    {"operation": "servicenow", "status": "completed", "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67"},
    {"operation": "pagerduty", "status": "completed", "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c"},
    {"operation": "argo_verify", "status": "failed", "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a"},
    {"operation": "audit_bundle", "status": "completed", "operationIdempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:12473c4dca6b30d5a32263bfd095119c"}
  ],
  "compensationRecords": [],
  "auditOutbox": {
    "operation": "audit_bundle",
    "status": "completed",
    "sideEffectId": "audit:347249",
    "idempotencyKey": "side-effect:v1:12473c4dca6b30d5a32263bfd095119c"
  },
  "auditEventId": "audit-event:<first-32-hex-of-semantic-bundle-hash>",
  "auditBundleHash": "<full-64-hex-semantic-bundle-hash>"
}

auditBundleHash is SHA-256 over the recursively sorted audit bundle after removing volatile fields such as generated timestamps, stage timings, claim ids, retry counters, and the hash fields themselves. auditEventId prefixes the first 32 hex characters of that full digest. This makes replay of the same semantic record stable while preserving full component timestamps in the stored document.

When Postgres appends the event, it computes a second hash:

{
  "algorithm": "sha256-canonical-json-v1",
  "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
  "auditEventId": "audit-event:<semantic-id>",
  "bundleHash": "<full-audit-bundle-hash>",
  "previousEventHash": "audit-chain:genesis",
  "document": "<complete stamped audit document without auditChain>"
}

The full SHA-256 of that canonical object becomes aiops_audit_events.event_hash. The next event copies it into previous_event_hash, producing a per-audit-case tamper-evident chain.

Read this as: Kestra retains the returned audit body as its final flow output. The gateway writes the latest projection to aiops_audit_records(idempotency_key=‘audit:v1:9c3c…’) and an immutable event to aiops_audit_events(audit_event_id=‘audit-event:…’). The audit operation key remains inside the document and its own outbox row. These tables, not the Kestra success badge, determine whether the record is complete.

4.12 What appears in logs, metrics, and traces

The gateway exposes OpenTelemetry spans and Prometheus metrics using bounded, sanitized attributes. Raw payloads, credentials, kubeconfig, and provider response bodies should not become metric labels or span attributes.

Signal Useful dimensions Question answered
HTTP request duration/count Normalized route, method, status Which gateway contract is slow or failing?
Planner calls Provider and outcome Is Claude succeeding, cached, unavailable, or budget-limited?
Planner decision quality Provider, model mode, decision, outcome What decisions are being produced?
Policy decisions Allow/deny and violation count Are alerts being blocked, and why?
Approval decisions Mode, event type, reason, time to decision Where is human authorization waiting?
Side effects Operation and outcome Which provider operation created, replayed, recovered, or failed?
Outbox gauge Backend, operation, status Is recoverable or ambiguous work accumulating?
Provider exceptions Operation, phase, exception class, status class Did failure occur before write, after write, or during readback?
Trace span http, workflow, side_effect, provider operation Which internal stage consumed the time?

The operational debugging order is trace first, then the matching Postgres attempt/outbox rows, then the external provider object. Logs describe what the process observed; Postgres and provider readback determine durable truth.

5. Postgres: what is stored

Kestra and the gateway should use separate schemas even when they share the same managed Postgres instance:

Schema Owner Typical content
kestra_runtime Kestra Executions, task runs, queues, triggers, logs
aiops_control_plane Planner gateway Attempts, workflow components, outbox, audit, compensation, control flags

Gateway tables

Table Key Purpose Mutation model
aiops_remediation_attempts attempt_key Slim relational ledger joining one exact attempt to case, audit case, catalog, and terminal status Created with a compact stamped document; terminal metadata advances at audit
aiops_state_records (category, idempotency_key) Server-issued evidence, plan, policy, approval, provider state Insert/read/update by category contract
aiops_outbox_records record_key; generated attempt_key and operation_key Durable intent and provider-call state machine Compare-and-set transitions and claim lease
aiops_compensation_records record_key Later-stage failure after an earlier effect Recovery status advances explicitly
aiops_audit_records idempotency_key equal to audit_case_key Latest reconstruction projection for one alert-to-scope audit case Upserted projection
aiops_audit_events audit_event_id Append-only evidence history Insert only; previous/event hash chain

Live sandbox cross-check: exact identifiers from one execution

The reference fixture in the remainder of this section is intentionally stable and easy to recompute. The following specimen is different: it is an exact read-only capture from a live managed-Kubernetes sandbox on 13 July 2026. No active credential is included.

Live fact Exact observed value
Kestra execution 2Vd0ZnYLHkgY5R5ukY4bIS
Namespace / flow / revision aiops.kestra / catalog_remediation_aiops / 11
Execution state SUCCESS after 28.806247 seconds; one RETRYING history transition
Acceptance run id failure-depth-final-20260713T001300Z-provider-after-write-recovery
Source Grafana fingerprint 3755d09eabf9ace0
Planner mode for this sandbox gate deterministic-fallback, llmUsed=false
Approval Manual approval 1921bcbc82c56c5f56a381e2
GitHub result Draft PR 291, branch aiops-mimir-compactor-pvc-pressure-9d9922b84da0
ITSM result HTTP simulator object chg-a4b3eac739bb, recovered by correlation after an injected response failure
Incident result HTTP simulator object inc-ca8c8e01d514
