Argo result awaiting_pr_merge_revision; read-only verification not yet green
Audit result Reconstruction complete; event and chain hashes shown below

This live gate deliberately ran without an LLM because the test invariant was side-effect recovery, not proposal quality. The plan still passed through the same typed plan, OPA, approval, preflight, outbox, GitHub, Argo, and audit contracts. The Anthropic-mode reference response in section 4.3 shows the model provider boundary used when LLM_MODE=anthropic.

What Kestra persisted for the live execution

The row kestra_runtime.executions.id='2Vd0ZnYLHkgY5R5ukY4bIS' contains the trigger and a taskRunList. Each HTTP task stores the URI, status code, response headers, and the complete gateway response string under outputs.body.

Task Gateway URI HTTP Task duration Kestra attempts
collect_evidence http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/evidence 200 0.148 s 1
planner http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/plan 200 0.120 s 1
policy http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/policy 200 0.150 s 1
request_approval http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/approval/request 200 0.183 s 1
provider_preflight http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/providers/preflight 200 0.153 s 1
create_gitops_pr http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/gitops/pr 200 9.148 s 1
notify_provider_sinks http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/providers/notify 200 16.388 s 2
verify_argo_readonly http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/argo/verify 200 0.555 s 1
audit http://planner-gateway:8090/v1/audit 200 0.387 s 1
WarningKestra runtime retention is part of the threat model

The live executions.value.trigger object retained the webhook body and request headers. This public trace removes the authorization header. Production ingress must use a narrowly scoped, rotatable webhook credential; restrict and encrypt the Kestra database; set explicit execution retention; and verify whether the chosen ingress path can strip credential headers before trigger persistence. The gateway’s final audit intentionally retains only bounded derived evidence, but that does not erase Kestra’s independent trigger history.

Live alert and composite identities

The live delivery contained one alert, so the reader-formatted source JSON value and digest were:

[
  "native:3755d09eabf9ace0"
]
SHA-256  b44c7c9c23b88aa5d613102acf99afc7d88b1339669932b04837429e75641d3b
key      alert:v1:b44c7c9c23b88aa5d613102acf99afc7

The server-issued evidence then linked that episode to the live catalog target:

Identity layer Exact live key First durable location
Alert episode alert:v1:b44c7c9c23b88aa5d613102acf99afc7 aiops_state_records(evidence).document.identity
Remediation scope remediation:v1:47c3df7d39dac9cb51fb2ebbcc3ffa67 Same evidence identity
Recurring case case:v1:e63cc2e33a9ea465d3f04b64657fd651 Evidence, then aiops_remediation_attempts.case_key
Alert-to-scope audit case audit:v1:5ae4172bd5a905850c98d600d87f1cd1 State-record key, attempt column, audit key
Exact change intent change-intent:v1:3495e613f4faacd7bb6d547de08ebc35 Approval binding and attempt/outbox identity context
Remediation attempt attempt:v1:136f997f160478dee49c905e388b0912 Attempt primary key and generated child columns

Every external operation is derived from the same attempt plus a distinct operation name. For example, GitHub uses this canonical JSON value:

{
  "attemptId": "attempt:v1:136f997f160478dee49c905e388b0912",
  "operation": "gitops_pr",
  "schemaVersion": "aiops.identity.v1"
}
Operation Full live SHA-256 Versioned operation key Derived outbox id
GitHub PR c6afb84f238aabd865d41c2867ab0ea60f25d32b87519f8765f55348a51a8aa3 side-effect:v1:c6afb84f238aabd865d41c2867ab0ea6 9d9922b84da00ccff01df179
ServiceNow e75b1d8ed0d3c17aa4118f982b2eb9d838df32853a7f8b415f98c08e704a7f0c side-effect:v1:e75b1d8ed0d3c17aa4118f982b2eb9d8 a4b3eac739bba0b40db9265e
PagerDuty 48929246bfd0fdfc33935537f69d39435dd22e0bb91d0dc6be063800465c87c8 side-effect:v1:48929246bfd0fdfc33935537f69d3943 ca8c8e01d5140653561df755
Argo verify fea113deac551481e101f78e861856be64187e7fede49d133c53f1282abac532 side-effect:v1:fea113deac551481e101f78e861856be 4af50d6854a87228ef272814
Audit bundle bb21f29c1559aca4031c347e6d57087361eff1e67cabd3b7ad981e2f2300448e side-effect:v1:bb21f29c1559aca4031c347e6d570873 13cbcd55836d2bac5201b923

outboxId is a second deterministic hash: the first 24 hex characters of SHA-256 over the complete versioned operation-key string. The GitHub branch suffix uses the first 12 characters of the same second hash, which is why operation ...c6afb84f... produced outbox 9d9922... and branch suffix 9d9922b84da0.

Live gateway rows and the recovery proof

The live attempt table contained a slim spine, exactly as declared by the source schema:

{
  "attempt_key": "attempt:v1:136f997f160478dee49c905e388b0912",
  "case_key": "case:v1:e63cc2e33a9ea465d3f04b64657fd651",
  "audit_case_key": "audit:v1:5ae4172bd5a905850c98d600d87f1cd1",
  "catalog_entry_id": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "terminal_status": "complete",
  "document": {
    "attemptKey": "attempt:v1:136f997f160478dee49c905e388b0912",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:e63cc2e33a9ea465d3f04b64657fd651",
    "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:5ae4172bd5a905850c98d600d87f1cd1",
    "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
    "terminalStatus": "complete",
    "documentVersion": "aiops.remediation-attempt.v1"
  }
}

The outbox rows captured independent outcomes rather than one misleading global transaction:

Operation Status Provider attempts Replays Latest outcome Provider object
gitops_pr completed 1 0 created pr:291
servicenow completed 2 51 provider_recovery_found_existing_side_effect chg-a4b3eac739bb
pagerduty completed 1 0 triggered_or_updated inc-ca8c8e01d514
argo_verify failed 0 0 read_only_verification_not_yet_green none yet
audit_bundle completed 0 0 audit_bundle_written audit:332507

The ServiceNow simulator accepted the write and then returned an injected server error. Kestra retried notify_provider_sinks; the gateway read by correlation key and recovered chg-a4b3eac739bb. The high attemptCount=56 and replayCount=51 reflect repeated recovery-gate exercises against the same durable operation, not 56 provider writes. providerAttemptCount=2 is the bounded provider-call count for the final durable row.

The real audit rows closed the linkage with full hashes:

{
  "aiops_audit_records.idempotency_key": "audit:v1:5ae4172bd5a905850c98d600d87f1cd1",
  "auditEventId": "audit-event:a45696d143224c253af7947ec0704605",
  "auditBundleHash": "a45696d143224c253af7947ec070460547066dccde7d9a0914da1f11ec4bc664",
  "aiops_audit_events.previous_event_hash": "audit-chain:genesis",
  "aiops_audit_events.event_hash": "27b2f51dbf14afc6300d09ff429bbbd4831d0d446aae394a852c9507af07f567"
}
ImportantThree statuses answer three different questions

Kestra SUCCESS means the workflow reached a defined terminal output. Audit complete means the required durable components were reconstructable. Argo awaiting_pr_merge_revision means the proposed change is not deployed or verified yet. None of those statements substitutes for the others.

Reference-fixture deterministic identity values

These values are not invented display placeholders. They were produced by the repository’s current identity.py functions using the exact Grafana fixture, catalog entry, plan, and OPA result printed in this document. A different cell, target, proposed value, or policy digest intentionally produces different keys.

Name Concrete value Durable use
Alert episode alert:v1:2cacc6298d7fd96fec6e4296e8dc5794 Source episode correlation
Remediation scope remediation:v1:ee5b4d4297b1242f6dfd9b33ec6217b4 Catalog target + topology
Operational case case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3 Recurring failure case
Audit case audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b Evidence, plan, and policy component key
Change intent change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8 Exact stable change authorization input
Remediation attempt attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf Approval, preflight, and referential spine
GitHub PR operation side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6 Outbox row and GitHub correlation
ServiceNow operation side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67 Independent ITSM idempotency scope
PagerDuty operation side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c Independent incident idempotency scope
Argo verification operation side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a Independent readback scope
Audit operation side-effect:v1:12473c4dca6b30d5a32263bfd095119c Audit side-effect outbox scope; the projection itself is keyed by audit case

What is inserted after each stage

After gateway route Table and key Concrete material written Why it is durable
/v1/evidence aiops_state_records / (evidence, audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b) Normalized topology, source fingerprint, GitOps current value 200Gi, redaction flags Later stages load a server-issued component rather than trusting a new raw alert body
/v1/plan aiops_state_records / (plans, audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b) Typed propose_gitops_pr plan, 200Gi -> 300Gi, directMutation=false, model metadata Policy and approval bind the exact normalized plan
/v1/policy aiops_state_records / (policies, audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b) decision=allow, OPA digest, empty violations, PR-only boundary A later side effect cannot substitute a different policy result
/v1/approval/request aiops_remediation_attempts / attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf and aiops_state_records / (approvals, attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf) Compact attempt spine in the attempt table; full binding hashes, issuer, expiry, and decision in the approval component Approval is not a free Boolean and cannot authorize another attempt
/v1/gitops/pr before GitHub aiops_outbox_records / side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6 intent_recorded, operation, request-scope digest, authorization binding, attempt identity A process crash cannot erase the fact that the provider call was intended
/v1/gitops/pr after GitHub Same outbox row completed, side_effect_id=pr:82, branch, PR number, draft state Identical retries return the recorded provider object
/v1/audit aiops_audit_records(idempotency_key=auditCaseKey) plus aiops_audit_events(audit_event_id) Latest reconstruction projection and append-only event with previous/event hashes Operators can reconstruct both current status and ordered audit history

Attempt spine row

The relational columns support joins and foreign keys. This table deliberately keeps a compact stamped document; the full typed evidence, plan, policy, approval, and provider documents belong in aiops_state_records.

{
  "attempt_key": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
  "case_key": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
  "audit_case_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
  "catalog_entry_id": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
  "terminal_status": "complete",
  "document": {
    "attemptKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "caseKey": "case:v1:6b0fa0d60c77c1ac5a9202a01c05b4d3",
    "auditCaseKey": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
    "terminalStatus": "complete",
    "documentVersion": "aiops.remediation-attempt.v1"
  }
}

Before final audit, the same row normally has terminal_status=null and terminalStatus=null. Audit upserts the terminal status in the same transaction that appends the audit event; it does not replace this slim ledger with the complete audit bundle.

Server-issued component rows

The primary key is (category, idempotency_key). Evidence, plan, and policy use the audit case key because they describe the alert episode and remediation scope before an approved attempt exists. Approval uses the attempt key.

[
  {
    "category": "evidence",
    "idempotency_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "document": {
      "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
      "cluster": "contextual-cellgroup-a",
      "cellId": "contextual-cell-a",
      "namespace": "mimir",
      "workload": "mimir-compactor",
      "pvc": "mimir-compactor-data",
      "sensitiveDataIncluded": false,
      "gitopsAuthoritativeCurrent": {
        "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
        "value": "200Gi"
      },
      "documentVersion": "aiops.state-record.v1"
    }
  },
  {
    "category": "plans",
    "idempotency_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "document": {
      "decision": "propose_gitops_pr",
      "proposedChange": {
        "action": "increase_pvc_size",
        "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
        "currentValue": "200Gi",
        "proposedValue": "300Gi",
        "directMutation": false
      },
      "documentVersion": "aiops.state-record.v1"
    }
  },
  {
    "category": "policies",
    "idempotency_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "document": {
      "decision": "allow",
      "policyEngine": "opa",
      "violations": [],
      "enforcedBoundary": "gitops_pull_request",
      "documentVersion": "aiops.state-record.v1"
    }
  },
  {
    "category": "approvals",
    "idempotency_key": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "document": {
      "approved": true,
      "idempotencyKey": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
      "identityContext": {
        "changeIntentHash": "change-intent:v1:724ed65eab3197d755832a61f894bad8",
        "remediationAttemptId": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf"
      },
      "documentVersion": "aiops.state-record.v1"
    }
  }
]

GitHub outbox row: three snapshots of the same row

The row key does not change as ownership and provider truth advance. The generated attempt_key and operation_key columns are derived from the JSONB identity context and constrained by Postgres.

{
  "record_key": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
  "idempotency_key": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
  "attempt_key": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
  "operation_key": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
  "operation": "gitops_pr",
  "status": "intent_recorded",
  "side_effect_id": null,
  "document": {
    "preSideEffectRecorded": true,
    "requestScopeHash": "73d4bc190257f0be90d95903b892ceed2ca184b99f6bbc9da1b27464cfeb0b33",
    "authorizationBindingHash": "faec972905216e8bb5c95975b502b8d200221b5e2ce05153fec393ad19cd446f",
    "attemptCount": 1,
    "providerAttemptCount": 0,
    "replayCount": 0
  }
}
{
  "record_key": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
  "status": "provider_call_claimed",
  "document": {
    "claimId": "claim-7f31",
    "claimedAt": "2026-07-12T16:05:00Z",
    "claimExpiresAt": "2026-07-12T16:05:30Z",
    "providerAttemptCount": 1
  }
}
{
  "record_key": "side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6",
  "status": "completed",
  "side_effect_id": "pr:82",
  "document": {
    "latestOutcome": "created",
    "prNumber": 82,
    "requiresHumanApproval": true,
    "autoMerge": false,
    "branch": "aiops-mimir-compactor-pvc-pressure-e5961d0476b1",
    "targetPath": "clusters/contextual-cellgroup-a/namespaces/mimir/mimir/values.yaml",
    "field": "compactor.persistentVolume.size",
    "currentValue": "200Gi",
    "proposedValue": "300Gi",
    "directMutation": false
  }
}
ImportantThese are lifecycle snapshots, not three inserts

Postgres contains one GitHub outbox row for this operation key. Compare-and-set updates advance it from intent_recorded to provider_call_claimed to completed. A second replica observes the same row; it does not insert a second provider operation.

Latest audit projection and append-only event

{
  "aiops_audit_records": {
    "idempotency_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "attempt_key": "attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf",
    "audit_case_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "document": {
      "auditStatus": "complete",
      "catalogEntryId": "mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure",
      "gitopsPr": {"prNumber": 82, "requiresHumanApproval": true},
      "auditCompletenessViolations": [],
      "auditEventId": "<runtime-derived-audit-event-id>",
      "auditBundleHash": "<runtime-derived-full-64-hex-hash>"
    }
  },
  "aiops_audit_events": {
    "audit_event_id": "<same-runtime-derived-audit-event-id>",
    "idempotency_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "audit_case_key": "audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b",
    "bundle_hash": "<same-runtime-derived-full-64-hex-hash>",
    "previous_event_hash": "audit-chain:genesis",
    "event_hash": "<full-64-hex-chain-event-hash>",
    "document": {"auditStatus": "complete", "auditChain": {"algorithm": "sha256-canonical-json-v1"}}
  }
}

The placeholders in this reference-fixture block are runtime outputs because the complete audit document includes the actual component timestamps and provider results. The live-sandbox block below supplies real values for all three hashes.

Representative rows for this case

Table Key fragment Important fields
aiops_remediation_attempts attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf case_key, audit_case_key, catalog_entry_id, terminal status
aiops_state_records evidence / audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b Normalized topology, current GitOps value, redaction status
aiops_state_records plans / audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b Typed proposal and provider/model metadata
aiops_state_records policies / audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b Allow/deny decision, violations, policy-bundle digest
aiops_state_records approvals / attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf Approval id, binding hashes, issuer, expiry, terminal decision
aiops_outbox_records gitops_pr / side-effect:v1:e9b366fc109fedbbf9507a3ff6c19ae6 intent_recorded -> provider_call_claimed -> completed, PR number and URL
aiops_outbox_records servicenow / side-effect:v1:8d4fcad143b6a0cd32d4150573c41a67 Correlation id and readback result
aiops_outbox_records pagerduty / side-effect:v1:4cef792654ee6f7ec8c15890f868629c Dedupe key and readback result
aiops_outbox_records argo_verify / side-effect:v1:1de5cbebecbea20f0c7b2a02e84bc13a Application, expected revision, observed sync/health
aiops_audit_records audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b Latest projection keyed by audit case; contains the audit operation key in its document
aiops_audit_events audit-event:<semantic-bundle-hash-prefix> Bundle hash, previous event hash, event hash, timestamp

Useful forensic queries

-- One attempt and its identity spine
select attempt_key, case_key, audit_case_key, catalog_entry_id, document
from aiops_control_plane.aiops_remediation_attempts
where catalog_entry_id = 'mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure'
order by created_at desc
limit 1;

-- Every external operation for that attempt
select operation, operation_key, status, side_effect_id, updated_at, document
from aiops_control_plane.aiops_outbox_records
where attempt_key = 'attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf'
order by updated_at;

-- Approval component as issued by the gateway
select category, idempotency_key, document
from aiops_control_plane.aiops_state_records
where category = 'approvals'
  and idempotency_key = 'attempt:v1:c6a3967e3d020c213af8697c45487dbf';

-- Tamper-evident audit chain for this case
select audit_event_id, previous_event_hash, event_hash, bundle_hash, created_at
from aiops_control_plane.aiops_audit_events
where audit_case_key = 'audit:v1:9c3cff8c38974c415bdad7075e4cbc7b'
order by created_at, audit_event_id;

6. What happens when something fails

Failure point Expected terminal behavior External write allowed? Recovery source
Webhook key invalid Kestra rejects ingress No Grafana retry/contact-point error
Evidence contract invalid Kestra execution fails before policy No Kestra execution log
Topology missing or ambiguous Policy denies or routes to manual review No Audit/policy violations
Claude unavailable Fail closed, or use explicitly configured deterministic mode only outside production policy No by default Planner error and metrics
Claude returns direct kubectl Policy denies No Policy violations
Plan changes after approval Approval binding mismatch No Server-issued approval record
Approval expires Authorization returns stale No Approval record and clock metadata
GitHub returns 500 before write Outbox remains retryable under bounded backoff Not confirmed Outbox attempt history
GitHub accepts PR but response is lost Mark ambiguous; read back deterministic branch/PR identity Do not create again until readback GitHub + outbox correlation
Gateway pod dies after intent Another replica claims after lease expiry At most one active claimant Postgres outbox lease
Provider notify fails after PR Record compensation/recovery work; do not delete PR automatically Existing PR remains Compensation + sweep
Argo is healthy at wrong revision Verification fails revision binding No new mutation Argo Application readback
Audit append fails Attempt is not declared fully complete No duplicate provider write Durable operation records + sweep

7. The invariants

These invariants define the system’s safety contract. They are more important than a particular class or YAML task.

Invariant Concrete enforcement
No direct production mutation Catalog boundary, OPA rules, directMutation: false, PR-only provider, read-only Argo client
The model cannot authorize Claude returns PlanComponent; OPA, approval, preflight, and provider credentials remain outside the model
Approval binds the exact intent Hashes cover case, catalog, evidence, plan, policy, target/value intent, and attempt; stale or changed input is rejected
Durable intent precedes external effect Outbox row is inserted before GitHub, ITSM, incident, Argo, or audit provider work
One operation converges to one provider object Versioned operation key, unique durable row, claim lease, deterministic provider correlation, and readback
Ambiguity is not treated as failure Accepted-write/lost-response enters unknown external state and requires readback before retry
Audit is reconstructed from trusted state Server-issued components, outbox records, latest projection, and append-only hash-chained events
Human merge remains the deployment boundary Draft PR, branch protection, CODEOWNERS/review, no self-merge, Argo reconciles only approved Git
Healthy is revision-specific Argo verification must match expected revision; generic Synced/Healthy is insufficient
PVC recovery is workload-visible Verify bound PVC and filesystem capacity, not only Helm values or StatefulSet templates

8. What the reconciliation sweep actually does

The sweep calls:

GET /v1/reconciliation/sweep
X-AIOPS-Gateway-Token: <authorized read token>

It queries durable state for:

Worklist Examples
Recoverable outbox records failed, unknown_external_state, expired claims
Outstanding compensation PR exists but later provider/audit stage did not converge
Truncation and backlog signals More recoverable rows exist than the response limit

It does not run PromQL to detect PVC pressure and does not infer the catalog case. Grafana performs detection. The sweep is a control-plane repair loop for operations the gateway already knows about.

9. Minimal live walkthrough

Use this order when showing or debugging the system:

  1. Open Grafana alert rule and contact point; show the target Kestra webhook.
  2. Open the Kestra flow trigger and one execution; identify the nine gateway tasks.
  3. Inspect the incoming webhook fixture and the collect_evidence request body.
  4. Inspect evidence, plan, policy, and approval outputs before looking at the PR.
  5. Query aiops_remediation_attempts to obtain the attempt_key.
  6. Query all aiops_outbox_records for that attempt; verify one completed row per operation.
  7. Open the draft GitHub PR; show the single 200Gi -> 300Gi field change and branch protection.
  8. Open the Argo Application; distinguish PR observation from post-merge revision verification.
  9. Query aiops_audit_events; verify the previous/event hash chain.
  10. Replay the same webhook; confirm the same operation keys and PR are returned.

10. Source map

Concern Source
Canonical workflow and webhook trigger flows/catalog_remediation_aiops.yml
Exact Grafana event fixtures/grafana-webhook-mimir-compactor.json
Compactor operational contract planner-gateway/catalog/remediations/mimir.compactor.pvc-pressure.yaml
HTTP request/response schemas planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/contracts.py
FastAPI routing and blocking-call offload planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/http_server.py
Workflow endpoint behavior planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/workflow/operations.py
Alert, topology, case, attempt, and operation identity planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/identity.py and identity_context.py
Approval issuance and terminal manual decisions planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/workflow/approvals.py
Side-effect authorization planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/workflow/authorization.py
OPA policy planner-gateway/policies/aiops_policy.rego
Transactional outbox executor planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/side_effects.py
Durable state transitions planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/state_store.py and postgres_repository.py
Database schema planner-gateway/alembic/versions/
Argo read-only adapter planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/providers/argo.py
GitHub provider and YAML patch planner-gateway/aiops_control_plane/providers/github.py and gitops_patch.py
Recovery worklist flows/reconciliation_sweep_aiops.yml and state_backend.py

One-sentence description: Grafana detects an operational condition; Kestra sequences typed gateway calls; Claude proposes one catalog-bounded GitOps change; OPA and a bound human approval authorize it; Postgres makes provider effects recoverable and idempotent; GitHub and Argo remain the change and deployment systems of record.
